articles.title: European Aluminium calls for indirect ban on Russian aluminium imports
articles.summary: European Aluminium launched its #NoSecondPassport campaign calling for an enforceable indirect ban on Russian primary aluminium in the EU's next sanctions package, due for approval on 15 July 2026. The campaign highlights a loophole whereby Russian metal processed in third countries—Turkey being the primary route, sourcing ~20% of its primary aluminium from Russia while supplying ~55% of EU extruded aluminium imports—re-enters the EU market as semi-finished products, sustaining around $10bn in annual Russian export revenues.
articles.novelis_angle: Closing this loophole would reduce competitive pressure on European FRP producers from products made with sanctioned Russian metal, strengthening relative demand for low-carbon, closed-loop aluminium where Novelis holds a structural advantage.

articles.title: US Customs to auction 12,000 tonnes of seized aluminium imported from China
articles.summary: US Customs and Border Protection will hold a final public auction of approximately 12,000 tonnes of 6000-series extruded aluminium profiles on 23 July 2026 in Riverside, California, closing a nearly decade-long anti-dumping investigation into imports linked to China Zhongwang Holdings. The material—stored across more than 600 shipping containers—was seized in 2017 after being imported as non-functional aluminium pallets to circumvent US tariffs on finished aluminium products.

articles.title: EGA reports ahead-of-schedule progress in Al Taweelah aluminium smelter restart, targets pre-incident production within a year
articles.summary: Emirates Global Aluminium confirmed on 2 July that 89 of Al Taweelah's 1,262 reduction cells are back in operation following the 28 March attack, with bath cleaning approximately 90% complete and frozen metal cleared from over 20% of cells. EGA targets a return to pre-incident output within a year, with recovery running ahead of its internal schedule; first alumina production from the Al Taweelah refinery is expected early in Q3 2026.
articles.novelis_angle: Faster-than-expected EGA recovery signals a gradual easing of the P1020 supply tightness that drove European duty-paid premium spikes in Q2 2026, improving metal availability for Novelis's European rolling mills over the next 12 months.

articles.title: Alcoa to acquire South32's bauxite, alumina and aluminium assets for $4.1bn
articles.summary: Alcoa announced a definitive agreement on 30 June to acquire South32's bauxite, alumina and aluminium smelting operations in Australia, South Africa and Brazil for $3.1bn cash plus ~17m new shares, totalling $4.1bn upfront with a $750m contingent value right linked to alumina and aluminium prices through 2030. The deal, targeting H1 2027 close, would make Alcoa the world's second-largest primary aluminium producer at approximately 3.4Mt/yr of combined smelting capacity.
articles.novelis_angle: A more vertically integrated Alcoa at this scale could reshape long-run P1020 supply dynamics globally; further consolidation of the primary sector may affect metal sourcing costs and availability for European FRP producers including Novelis as the deal closes in 2027.
